summary: Advanced Sysdolphin baselib integration into the DTK object/split setup, with matching status updates for several modules, a matched debug.c relocation out of synth.s, rumble API renames and struct cleanup, incomplete psdisp C work, and a new match for splGetHelmite. The PR also standardized known-bug comments to Doxygen-style @bug annotations and propagated corrected HSD_PadRumble and debug callback symbol names through assembly references and symbols.txt.
what_changed: Added Sysdolphin baselib objects to the DTK build configuration, including matching objects such as dobj.c, initialize.c, video.c, rumble.c, util.c, fog.c, list.c, object.c, debug.c, and nonmatching objects such as psdisp.c, psdisptev.c, psappsrt.c, sobjlib.c, and sislib.c.

Expanded config/GALE01/splits.txt with section ranges for many Sysdolphin baselib files, notably rumble.c, debug.c, psdisp.c, psdisptev.c, psappsrt.c, and surrounding modules; psdisp entries include extab and extabindex ranges.
Moved the two tiny callback setter functions and related sbss symbols out of asm/sysdolphin/baselib/synth.s by matching them in debug.c as HSD_SetReportCallback and HSD_SetPanicCallback.

Renamed debug symbols from address-based HSD_Debug_8038815C and HSD_Debug_803881E4 to report_func and HSD_LogInit, introduced ReportCallback, PanicCallback, and LogFunc typedefs, and changed initialize.c to call HSD_LogInit.

Renamed many address-based rumble functions to semantic HSD_PadRumble names, including HSD_PadRumbleOffN, HSD_PadRumbleFree, HSD_PadRumbleRemove, HSD_PadRumbleRemoveAll, HSD_PadRumbleRemoveId, HSD_PadRumblePauseAll, HSD_PadRumbleUnpauseAll, HSD_PadRumbleAdd, and HSD_PadRumbleInterpret1.
Cleaned rumble struct usage by replacing duplicated Struct804C22E0 usage with HSD_RumbleData and removing the Struct804C22E0 forward declaration.
Updated asm callsites in gm_1601.s, gmcamera.s, lb_00F9.s, mnvibration.s, controller.s, and melee C callsites in ftcommon.c and ftlib.c to use the new HSD_PadRumble symbol names.
Renamed HSD_PSDisp_8039FC70 to particleSort in asm/sysdolphin/baselib/psdisp.s and extabindex.s, and exposed particleSort plus psdisp helper prototypes in psdisp.h.

Added incomplete psdisp C work behind #if 0, including setVtxDesc, calcTornadoLastPos, getColorPrimEnv, and getColorMatAmb sketches; one comment explicitly marks a suspected Melee bug in calcTornadoLastPos.
Adjusted psstructs.h particle layout evidence, including changing the documented _particle size from 0x9C to 0x94 and shifting/removing fields around rotate/color target data.
Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/spline.c with a matching splGetHelmite implementation.
Standardized known-bug comments in fighter.c and lbfile.c to Doxygen-style /// @bug comments.
Deleted synth.h after moving its callback globals/prototypes to debug-related naming and headers.

symbols.txt gained semantic names for multiple SDK/HSD symbols, including PADControlMotor, VI* functions, HSD_PadRumble* functions, report_func, HSD_SetReportCallback, HSD_SetPanicCallback, reportCallback, and panicCallback.
smart_moves: Fixed a bad split by recognizing that HSD_Synth_80388320 and HSD_Synth_80388328 were actually debug callback setters, then moving their code/data ownership from synth.s to debug.c and naming them by behavior.

Renamed rumble functions based on observed callsite behavior, making cross-system callsites in game mode, camera, controller, menu vibration, fighter, and lb code self-documenting without changing code generation intent.
Removed a duplicated rumble data struct instead of preserving parallel anonymous/address-based types, reducing future header drift and improving confidence in field names like listdatap and status.
Kept incomplete psdisp work isolated behind #if 0 while still recording prototypes and symbol names, preserving research without risking a nonmatching or behavior-changing replacement.
Updated extabindex when renaming particleSort, which avoids leaving exception table metadata pointing at obsolete address-style names.
Added explicit DTK split ranges and configure.py object classifications together, so build-system ownership and symbol ownership moved in lockstep.
decomp_lessons: When small assembly functions appear in the wrong module, check their global data targets and callsite semantics before accepting the file boundary; this PR reclassified synth.s callback setters as debug.c functions.
Semantic renames should be propagated through asm callsites, C callsites, symbols.txt, headers, and extab/extabindex references in the same PR to avoid mixed address-based and named symbol states.

For matching-oriented struct cleanup, prefer consolidating duplicated placeholder structs into the real domain type once field usage is known; here Struct804C22E0 was removed in favor of HSD_RumbleData.
Documenting known retail bugs with @bug comments is useful decomp hygiene: it separates intentional source reproduction from code that a normal rewrite would 'fix'.
Incomplete C reconstructions can still be useful if guarded and clearly marked; psdisp.c captured helper logic and a suspected bug without forcing the file to match.
DTK integration requires both object list changes and accurate split section ranges, including non-text sections and extab/extabindex when the module owns exception metadata.
Small polynomial/math matches like splGetHelmite may require preserving temporary structure and expression ordering rather than simplifying the formula.
naming_conventions: Replace address-suffixed unknown names with semantic HSD names when behavior is known: HSD_Rumble_80378208 became HSD_PadRumbleRemoveAll, HSD_Rumble_80378330 became HSD_PadRumblePauseAll, and HSD_Rumble_803783B0 became HSD_PadRumbleUnpauseAll.
Use HSD_PadRumble* for pad rumble APIs rather than generic HSD_Rumble_* when functions operate on pad rumble state.
Use callback names that describe stored function pointer roles: ReportCallback, PanicCallback, LogFunc, reportCallback, panicCallback, and logFunc.
Use HSD_LogInit for debug log hook initialization instead of an address-derived HSD_Debug_* name.
Rename psdisp helper HSD_PSDisp_8039FC70 to particleSort based on apparent role.
Use Doxygen-style /// @bug for known original-code bugs instead of ad hoc BUG comments.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted two matched 8-byte assembly functions from synth.s after reimplementing them in debug.c as direct stores to reportCallback and panicCallback.
Updated assembly branch labels/calls only at symbol-name level for rumble and debug callback functions, preserving branch targets while improving symbol knowledge.
Adjusted extabindex entry from HSD_PSDisp_8039FC70 to particleSort after the psdisp symbol rename.
Marked several configure.py Sysdolphin objects as Matching once corresponding split ownership and source matched, while leaving psdisp-related larger files as NonMatching.
Added splGetHelmite with explicit temporaries such as _3t2_T2, _2t3_T3, t3_T2, t2_T, t2, and _1_T2, indicating expression-shape preservation for matching.
Used #if 0 around incomplete psdisp helper implementations to keep research in-tree without compiling it into the matching build.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.

The PR body itself states the intent: make Sysdolphin matches part of the DTK setup, formalize @bug comments, fix rumble function names and struct usage, fix a bad synth/debug split, add incomplete psdisp work, and add the splGetHelmite match.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish or unguard psdisp.c helper functions such as setVtxDesc, calcTornadoLastPos, getColorPrimEnv, and getColorMatAmb.
Search for later changes to HSD_Rumble_80378524, which remained address-like after this rumble rename pass.
Check whether the _particle size/layout changes in psstructs.h were validated or revised in subsequent psdisp or psappsrt work.
Look for follow-up PRs that move psdisp.c, psdisptev.c, psappsrt.c, sobjlib.c, or sislib.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py.
Search for later cleanup of the @bug calcTornadoLastPos comment, especially the suspicious null check/access note.
